_comment: Hand-built fact graph for the Tier 0 floor test. Nodes encode atomic facts about a fictional setting (the 'Verdane Trade Union'). Multi-hop questions require combining 2-3 nodes. Fictional so the model cannot lean on training-data priors.

nodes.text: Verdane is a confederation of three city-states: Olara, Threnis, and Kvell.
Olara is the only Verdanian city-state with a deep-water port.
Threnis is landlocked and lies in the foothills of the Marrowback Mountains.
Kvell sits in the Yarrow Plains, between the Yarrow River and the southern coast.
The Verdane Trade Union levies a 4% tariff on all goods crossing between member city-states.
Goods imported from outside Verdane through Olara's port pay an additional 7% port duty before entering circulation.
Threnis is the sole Verdanian source of cinder-iron, mined exclusively in the Marrowback range.
Cinder-iron is required to forge resonant bells, a signature export of Kvell's foundries.
Kvell's foundries are the only producers of resonant bells in Verdane.
Verdanian resonant bells command premium prices in the Tyrn Archipelago, three weeks' sail from Olara.
The Tyrn Archipelago does not impose import tariffs on goods from confederations whose member states share a common currency.
All three Verdanian city-states use the silver kestrel as their common currency.
The Yarrow River runs from the Marrowback foothills through Kvell to the southern coast.
Riverboats can travel the full length of the Yarrow but cannot ascend the Marrowback foothills past the town of Renfall.
Renfall sits on the Threnis side of the Marrowback foothills, at the navigable head of the Yarrow River.
Cinder-iron from Threnian mines is hauled by mule-train down to Renfall, then loaded onto Yarrow riverboats bound for Kvell.
The Verdane Trade Union waives the 4% inter-city tariff on raw materials destined for Kvell's foundries.
Finished goods from Kvell, including resonant bells, do incur the 4% inter-city tariff when shipped to Olara for export.
Bells exported through Olara's port pay no port duty because Olara's port duty applies only to imports.
A standard Verdanian resonant bell sells for 240 silver kestrels at the Kvell foundry gate.
Tyrn Archipelago buyers typically pay 360 silver kestrels per bell delivered.
Olara's port duty rate of 7% is set by the Olaran Harbor Council, not the Trade Union.
The Olaran Harbor Council exempts goods produced within Verdane from port duty when those goods are being exported, not imported.
A typical mule-train can carry 80 stone of cinder-iron from a Marrowback mine to Renfall in two days.
One stone of cinder-iron is sufficient to forge three resonant bells.
Kvell's foundries collectively produce roughly 600 resonant bells per month under standard supply conditions.
Standard supply conditions assume mule-trains run between Threnis and Renfall five days a week.
If the Yarrow River freezes, riverboat traffic between Renfall and Kvell halts; this typically occurs for six weeks each winter.
